name: Restocking fee
summary: Fees deducted from refunds on returned products — typical 15-25% of purchase price.

evidence.claim: FTC Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule (16 CFR Part 435) requires sellers to clearly disclose return-and-refund policy including any restocking fee BEFORE the consumer commits to purchase.

body.description: Sellers withhold 15-25% of the original purchase price on returns of opened products, claiming inspection/repackaging costs. Best Buy waives for elite members but applies to others; many electronics retailers charge it on opened-box returns.

disclosureLegality.requirement: Must be clearly disclosed before purchase per FTC Mail Order Rule (E1).
California Civil Code §1723 requires return-policy disclosure within 5 business days of purchase or refund-by-default applies.

negotiability.script: I'm returning this within the return window. The product is unused/in original packaging. I'd like to request the restocking fee be waived since the product is in resalable condition.
